items.prompt: A free-running TB clock is usually generated by…
choices: An initial block with forever toggle and half-period delays
items.explain: forever #(PERIOD/2) clk = ~clk; is the classic generator pattern.

items.prompt: Active-low reset rst_n at zero means…
choices: Reset asserted — DUT held in reset
items.explain: rst_n low asserts reset; high deasserts for run phase.

items.prompt: Sync-style reset release for posedge flops often means…
choices: Deassert rst_n on or after a posedge clk
Toggle reset every half clock cycle randomly
Drive reset from a DUT output
items.explain: The classic starter holds two posedges then releases on an edge.

items.prompt: Releasing reset between clock edges (async) is always safe for every DUT.
items.explain: Some flops expect sync release; async mid-cycle can surprise posedge sampling.

items.prompt: repeat (N) @(posedge clk) while reset is asserted…
choices: Holds reset for N clock cycles before release
Removes the need for a clock generator
items.explain: Count posedges during assert phase to control reset length.
